---
title: "File conventions and directory structure"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{File conventions and directory structure}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This reference documents the file structure, naming conventions, and sidecar file formats used by docstyle. For workflow tutorials, see [Round-trip workflow](how-to-roundtrip.html).

## Directory structure

A typical docstyle project:

```
project/
├── _quarto.yml              # Project configuration
├── document.qmd             # Source document
├── styles.css               # Typography and colours
├── _docstyle/               # Sidecar directory (generated files)
│   ├── reference.docx       # Generated style template
│   ├── reference.docx.hash  # Cache invalidation hash
│   ├── comments.json        # Comment metadata
│   ├── revisions.json       # Track changes metadata
│   ├── references.json      # CSL-JSON bibliography
│   └── field-codes.json     # Zotero field codes
├── _extensions/             # Quarto extension (installed)
│   └── docstyle/
├── source/                  # Original Word documents (optional)
│   └── ORIGINAL_V1.0.docx
└── output/                  # Rendered documents
    └── document.docx
```

## Sidecar directory (`_docstyle/`)

The sidecar directory stores metadata that cannot be expressed in markdown but is needed for Word round-tripping. Configure with:

```yaml
docstyle:
  sidecar-dir: _docstyle    # Default location
```

### Why sidecar files?

Word stores rich metadata (author names, timestamps, comment threads) that markdown cannot represent. Rather than losing this data or cluttering QMD files, docstyle stores it in JSON sidecars:

- **QMD contains:** Lightweight spans like `[text]{.comment id="3"}`
- **JSON contains:** Full metadata (author: "Jane Smith", date: "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z")

This separation keeps QMD readable while preserving Word fidelity.

## Sidecar file formats

### `comments.json`

Stores comment metadata keyed by comment ID.

```json
{
  "0": {
    "id": "0",
    "author": "Doug Manuel",
    "date": "2025-12-11T17:10:00Z",
    "content": "Can you review this section?",
    "parent_id": null,
    "initials": "DGM"
  },
  "1": {
    "id": "1",
    "author": "Carol Bennett",
    "date": "2025-09-23T15:09:00Z",
    "content": "This needs clarification.",
    "parent_id": "0",
    "initials": "CB"
  }
}
```

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `id` | Unique identifier (matches QMD span) |
| `author` | Full name of commenter |
| `date` | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| `content` | Comment text |
| `parent_id` | ID of parent comment (for replies), or `null` |
| `initials` | Author initials shown in Word margin |

**QMD reference:**

```markdown
This [phrase]{.comment id="0"} has a comment attached.
```

### `revisions.json`

Stores track changes metadata keyed by revision ID.

```json
{
  "rev_10": {
    "id": "rev_10",
    "type": "insertion",
    "author": "Sarah Beach",
    "date": "2025-10-28T13:13:00Z",
    "content": "",
    "initials": "SB"
  },
  "rev_8": {
    "id": "rev_8",
    "type": "deletion",
    "author": "Sarah Beach",
    "date": "2025-10-28T13:13:00Z",
    "content": "deleted text here",
    "initials": "SB"
  }
}
```

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `id` | Unique identifier (format: `rev_N`) |
| `type` | `insertion` or `deletion` |
| `author` | Full name of editor |
| `date` | ISO 8601 timestamp |
| `content` | For deletions, the deleted text |
| `initials` | Author initials |

**QMD reference:**

```markdown
This [added text]{.ins id="rev_10"} was inserted.
This [~~removed text~~]{.del id="rev_8"} was deleted.
```

### `references.json`

CSL-JSON format bibliography. Used by `zotero-inject.lua` to restore Zotero citations.

```json
[
  {
    "id": "smith2023",
    "type": "article-journal",
    "title": "Example Article Title",
    "author": [
      {"family": "Smith", "given": "Jane"}
    ],
    "issued": {"date-parts": [[2023, 6, 15]]},
    "container-title": "Journal of Examples",
    "DOI": "10.1234/example.2023"
  }
]
```

This follows the standard CSL-JSON schema. Generated by `extract_citations()` or exported from Zotero.

### `field-codes.json`

Zotero field codes that encode citation metadata for round-tripping through Word.

```json
{
  "(Smith, 2023)": {
    "formatted": "(Smith, 2023)",
    "field_code": "ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION {...}",
    "citation_keys": ["smith2023"]
  }
}
```

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `formatted` | Display text of citation |
| `field_code` | Word field code (opaque to users) |
| `citation_keys` | Keys linking to `references.json` |

These are extracted during import and injected during render to preserve Zotero's citation management.

### `reference.docx`

Generated Word template containing styles from CSS. This is a binary file that should not be edited manually.

**Regeneration:** Delete `reference.docx` and `reference.docx.hash` to force regeneration on next render.

### `reference.docx.hash`

SHA-256 hash of CSS files used to generate `reference.docx`. Used for cache invalidation—if CSS changes, the reference doc is regenerated.

```
sha256:a1b2c3d4e5f6...
```

## QMD marker syntax

### Comment markers

docstyle supports two formats for comment markers in QMD:

**Span-based (legacy):**

```markdown
This [commented text]{.comment id="3"} appears in Word with a comment attached.
```

**HTML comment markers (robust):**

```markdown
This `<!-- comment:start id="3" -->`{=html}commented text`<!-- comment:end id="3" -->`{=html} spans complex structures.
```

The HTML format handles edge cases like comments spanning tracked changes or multiple formatting boundaries. Both render identically to Word.

### Revision markers

**Insertions:**

```markdown
This [inserted text]{.ins id="rev_7"} was added by a reviewer.
```

**Deletions:**

```markdown
This [~~deleted text~~]{.del id="rev_8"} was removed.
```

## Source directory (`source/`)

Optional directory for storing original Word documents. Useful for:

- Preserving the original import source
- Version comparison
- Audit trail

Configure with:

```yaml
docstyle:
  source-docx: "source/ORIGINAL_V1.0.docx"
```

## Output directory (`output/`)

Rendered documents go here. Configure at project level:

```yaml
project:
  output-dir: output
```

This directory is typically gitignored since outputs are reproducible from source.

## Git recommendations

### What to commit

```gitignore
# Commit these
_quarto.yml
*.qmd
*.css
_docstyle/comments.json
_docstyle/revisions.json
_docstyle/references.json
_docstyle/field-codes.json
source/                    # Original documents

# Gitignore these
output/                    # Reproducible from source
_docstyle/reference.docx   # Generated from CSS
_docstyle/reference.docx.hash
.quarto/
```

### Rationale

- **Commit sidecar JSON:** Preserves comment/revision/citation metadata
- **Gitignore reference.docx:** Binary file regenerated from CSS
- **Gitignore output/:** Reproducible artifacts

## File naming conventions

| Convention | Example | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| Version suffix | `DOCUMENT_V1.0.docx` | Track document versions |
| ISO dates | `2025-12-20` | Sortable, unambiguous |
| Underscores | `My_Document.qmd` | Avoid spaces in filenames |

## See also

- [YAML schema](reference-yaml-schema.html): Configuration options
- [Comments and revisions](comments-revisions.html): Workflow details
- [Zotero citations](zotero-citations.html): Citation round-trip
